questions.q:
2. For a continuous random variable X with a probability density function
```f(x) = k x³ ,  0 ≤ x ≤ 2
       0     ,  o.w.```
then the value of k is
questions.explanation:
For f(x) to be a valid PDF, ∫[0 to 2] kx³ dx = 1.
k × [x⁴/4] from 0 to 2 = k × 16/4 = 4k = 1, so k = 1/4.

questions.q:
3. The value of k when the probability mass function is given by:
```P(x) = x/k ,  x = 1, 2, 3, 4
        0   ,  o.w.```

4. A company producing electric relays has three manufacturing plants producing 50%, 30%, and 20%, respectively, of its product. Suppose that the probabilities that a relay manufactured by these plants is defective are 0.02, 0.05, and 0.01, respectively.

i) If a relay is selected at random from the output of the company, what is the probability that it is defective?
questions.explanation:
Using the Total Probability Theorem:
P(D) = P(D|B₁)P(B₁) + P(D|B₂)P(B₂) + P(D|B₃)P(B₃)
= (0.02)(0.5) + (0.05)(0.3) + (0.01)(0.2)
= 0.010 + 0.015 + 0.002
= 0.027

questions.q:
4. A company producing electric relays has three manufacturing plants producing 50%, 30%, and 20%, respectively, of its product. Suppose that the probabilities that a relay manufactured by these plants is defective are 0.02, 0.05, and 0.01, respectively.

ii) If a relay selected at random is found to be defective, what is the probability that it was manufactured by plant 2?
questions.explanation:
Using Bayes' Theorem:
P(B₂|D) = P(D|B₂) × P(B₂) / P(D)
= (0.05 × 0.3) / 0.027
= 0.015 / 0.027
= 5/9 ≈ 0.5556

This value does not match any of options a, b, or c, so the answer is: None of them.

If A ⊂ B, then Aᶜ ∩ B = B \ A, so P(Aᶜ ∩ B) = P(B) - P(A).
P(Aᶜ|B) = P(Aᶜ ∩ B) / P(B) = [P(B) - P(A)] / P(B) = 1 - P(A)/P(B).

If A = B, then P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B) = P(A)/P(A) = 1. But also P(A) = P(B) so P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B) = P(A)/P(B) = P(B)/P(B) = 1. More directly: since A = B, P(A|B) = P(B|A) = P(A)/P(B) = 1. Since both options P(B|A) and P(A) are circled/marked on the exam, the accepted answer is P(A) (= P(B) = 1 in this case).

questions.q: 8. If the knowledge that an event A has occurred implies that a second event B cannot occur, the events A and B are said to be
questions.explanation: If A occurring means B cannot occur, then A ∩ B = ∅, which is the definition of mutually exclusive events.

questions.q: 9. If A and B be independent events in a sample space S, then which of the following statement(s) are true
questions.explanation: If A and B are independent, then all pairs involving their complements are also independent: A & Bᶜ, Aᶜ & B, and Aᶜ & Bᶜ are all independent pairs.

questions.q:
10. A code consists of a digit chosen from 0 to 5 followed by a letter of the alphabet.
What is the probability the code is 5Z?
questions.explanation:
Digits from 0 to 5: 6 possible digits. Letters of the alphabet: 26 possible letters.
Total codes = 6 × 26 = 156.
P(code is 5Z) = 1/156.

questions.q: 11. If `A` and `B` are mutually exclusive events, then `p(B|A)` = ...
questions.explanation: If A and B are mutually exclusive, A ∩ B = ∅, so P(B|A) = P(A ∩ B)/P(A) = 0/P(A) = 0.

questions.q: 12. If `A` and `B` are independent events, Then `P(A|B)` = ...
questions.explanation: If A and B are independent, P(A ∩ B) = P(A)P(B), so P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B) = P(A)P(B)/P(B) = P(A).

questions.q: 13. The bag contains 5 red marbles, 4 green marbles and 1 blue marble. A marble is chosen at random from the bag and not replaced; then a second marble is chosen. What is the probability both marbles are green?
questions.explanation:
P(both green) = P(1st green) × P(2nd green | 1st green)
= (4/10) × (3/9)
= 12/90
= 2/15.

questions.q: 14. In Exton School, 60% of the boys play baseball and 24% of the boys play baseball and football. What percent of those that play baseball also play football?

questions.explanation:
If B ⊂ A, then every outcome in B is also in A, so A ∩ B = B.
P(A|B) = P(A ∩ B)/P(B) = P(B)/P(B) = 1.
